clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for ventilated patient review documentation.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common ICU / Critical Care documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct high-caution documentation workflow for clinician-stated assessment and plans around ventilated patient review documentation, without generating management advice.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
red_flag_prompts: airway, breathing, circulation, neurological, or sepsis concern documented if assessed
organ support/device context documented without management advice
urgent escalation discussion documented only if already performed

chips.text: Ventilated patient review documentation interval history documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
change since last review documented
severity/impact on function documented if discussed
associated symptoms reviewed if relevant
relevant negatives documented if assessed
patient concerns or goals documented if discussed
clinician-led risk context documented
scope limited to documentation scaffolding
Vital signs and monitoring documentation documented only if assessed
Systems-focused assessment documentation documented only if assessed
Lines/tubes/devices documentation if present documented only if assessed
Neurological status documentation documented only if assessed
airway, breathing, circulation, neurological, or sepsis concern documented if assessed
organ support/device context documented without management advice
urgent escalation discussion documented only if already performed
Monitoring data documented if reviewed by clinician
Laboratory trends documented if reviewed by clinician
Imaging and device reports documented if already available
clinician-entered documentation plan recorded
senior/specialist discussion documented if already performed
risk/consent discussion documented if clinician completed it
handover or follow-up documentation recorded if arranged by clinician

history_draft.text: Ventilated patient review documentation. [Interval history]. [Current symptoms/concerns]. [Change since last review]. [Relevant negatives]. [Clinician-documented discussion]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Vital signs and monitoring documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Systems-focused assessment documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Lines/tubes/devices documentation if present documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Neurological status documentation documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Monitoring data documented.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Laboratory trends documented.

clinical_blockers: Airway, indication, mode, settings, synchrony, oxygenation, ventilation, sedation, secretions, imaging, complications and goals remain unsupported.
All retained legacy items remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction is authorised.
